description:
'''Digicore''' (originally known as '''draincore''') is an [[Internet rap|Internet]] [[microgenre]] of [[hip-hop]] that developed alongside [[hyperpop]] in the late 2010s to early 2020s. The genre is typically made up of artists that share stylistic similarities to [[Drain Gang]] and [[100 gecs]], rather than the "[[bubblegum bass]]" musicians signed to the label [[PC Music]].   

During the late 2010s, the style initially emerged as a Drain Gang-inspired form of [[online rap]] music known as "draincore". In 2018, Dalton, a figure in the digicore scene, started a [[Minecraft]] and [[Discord]] server called "[[Loser's Club]]",which became a hub for several of the most popular artists within the digicore scene such as [[Quinn (musician)|Quinn]], Kmoe, [[Glaive (musician)|Glaive]], [[Ericdoa]] and [[Midwxst]]. By 2019, artist lonelee coined the term "digicore" to replace draincore. In 2020, the terms "[[robloxcore]]", "hyperpop" and "[[glitchcore]]" would be used synonymously with digicore until their styles perceptibly narrowed.   

By 2021, "digicore" was popularized by Billie Bugara's [[SoundCloud]] playlist of the same name. Artists Yungster Jack and [[David Shawty]] have been described as pioneers of the genre. Collectives such as [[helix tears]] and [[Novagang]] were influential to the style's development. Other notable acts include [[Jane Remover]], [[D0llywood1]], [[Whethan]], [[Aldn]], [[Quannnic]], [[Lucy Bedroque]] and [[Brakence]]. Digicore proved influential to several Internet microgenres such as [[HexD|hexd]], [[glitchcore]], [[sigilkore]], [[Jerk (music genre)|jerk]], [[hyperplugg]] and [[zoomergaze]].
